violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -4 cases of food on floor in walk in freezer.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Food removed from original packaging was not placed in a container with an impervious and nonabsorbent cover. -Croutons and salt on top of prep table without lids.--Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. -Bags of raw ground beef thawing on wire shelf above raw bacon and above unopened chubs of deli meats.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. -Packages of tuna thawing in 2 door make table were not removed from packaging per manufacturer label.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — ---Written procedure for hot hold of hot dogs not available. *Facility stated hot hold hot dogs are placed at approximately 11am and disposed of at 2 pm.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Multiple items were not properly date marked: Par cooked then thawed chick in 3 door table, deli meats in 2 door table, sliced turkey in 4 drawer unit, etc.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Compound butter and rice portions dated 5/7. *Stated that was the makeand freeze date but items were not re-dated for thaw date.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Food spill/debris at both compartments of alto sham. -Residue/debris at interior bottom of both oven interiors. -Residue/splatter front and handles of 3 door prep table. -Residue/splater at front of 2 door prep table. -Some splatter/residue at interior surfaces of microwave. -Some dust/debris at walk in cooler fans.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. -Kitchen handsink used as a dump sink - ice and flowers in basin upon arrival.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Large bowl of Caesar salad stored uncovered in the walk in cooler.--Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Multiple cases of food stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. -Multiple cases of food and bagged ice stored on the floor in the walk in freezer.

Reheating food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not reheated rapidly so all parts reached a temperature of 165F for 15 seconds within one hour. -Queso reheated on cook top at ~11:30a found in crock pot at ~12:10pm with temp of 109-158F. *Directed to return to double boil on cook top and finish reheat so all of product reaches 165F.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Rice dated 6/21 and sauce dated 6/29-Staff stated these portions were a different pull date from frozen and that the holding container date had not been changed. -Seasoned butter was not date marked.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Garlic paste (made in-house) dated 6/23.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Tongs at roller grill were left dirty from yesterday. -Food debris on pizza oven grates.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Some debris at interior bottoms of ovens. -Fronts and handles of cookline equipment have food splatter/residue. -Large make table interior shelves have grease/residue build up.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Water damaged ceiling tile above dish pit area walkway. -Kitchen ceiling tiles are acoustic tiles. (Ceiling tiles throughout kitchen were replaced over winter.)--No vent hood above high temp dishwasher in kitchen.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
Number, location, accessibility, soap and paper towels provided (3 pts deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
